quick_skim.title: PrimeKG-Plus: a literature-derived expansion of a multimodal precision medicine knowledge graph
quick_skim.abstract: Biomedical knowledge evolves rapidly, yet most disease-centered knowledge graphs remain unchanged after publication. We present PrimeKG-Plus, an extension of PrimeKG that updates all 20 original data resources to releases available as of Dec 2025 and incorporates additional resources, including OpenTargets, RepurposeDrugs, and nSIDES. The graph is further expanded with relations extracted from 637 PubMed abstracts and PMC full-text articles using a large-language-model-assisted curation workflow. Extracted relations were refined through normalization, UMLS-based synonym mapping, SapBERT embedding based similarity ranking, and human expert review to ensure data quality. This literature-driven expansion focuses on rare neurological disorders, including Canavan disease, Niemann-Pick disease type C, Tay-Sachs disease and Batten disease. Network topology analyses indicate that the expanded graph improves indirect drug-disease connectivity of 3-6 hops through the graph while adding 447,288 Drug-Protein-Disease paths linking drug-disease pairs that were previously unreachable. Temporal FDA validation captured 55 new molecular entities after the June 2021 PrimeKG data cut-off as drug nodes, 46 absent from the original PrimeKG. By integrating newly incorporated and updated resources with systematically curated literature-derived associations, PrimeKG-Plus provides an up-to-date knowledge graph for network-based drug repurposing and precision medicine applications. Data and code are publicly available.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: biomedical knowledge graph expansion for precision medicine
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用文献扩展生物医学知识图谱，可用于药物相互作用预测

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PrimeKG-Plus expands PrimeKG with updated data resources and literature-derived relations for precision medicine, offering a rich knowledge graph useful for drug-drug interaction prediction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前疾病中心知识图谱在发布后很少更新，限制了其时效性。本文提出PrimeKG-Plus，通过整合最新数据资源和利用大语言模型辅助从文献中提取关系，扩展了PrimeKG知识图谱。该图谱涵盖了药物、疾病、靶点等多模态信息，为药物相互作用预测等下游任务提供了丰富且高质量的知识支持。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当前疾病中心知识图谱在发布后很少更新，限制了其时效性。本文提出PrimeKG-Plus，通过整合最新数据资源和利用大语言模型辅助从文献中提取关系，扩展了PrimeKG知识图谱。该图谱涵盖了药物、疾病、靶点等多模态信息，为药物相互作用预测等下游任务提供了丰富且高质量的知识支持。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: biomedical knowledge graph for drug-drug interaction prediction

quick_skim.title: RetroAgent: Harnessing LLMs to Search Over Structured Memory for Agentic Retrosynthesis Planning
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-step retrosynthesis planning seeks to decompose a target molecule into commercially available building blocks through a sequence of feasible reactions. The vast combinatorial search space makes this task challenging even for expert chemists. Traditional methods combine tree search with offline-trained value networks that score candidates in isolation, without reasoning about complete multi-step routes. Recent work leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) for this task, but relies on simple interfaces that limit exploration of the full search space. We introduce RetroAgent, an LLM agent that bridges symbolic search and neural reasoning through a harness with structured memory. Through memory and chemistry tools, the agent observes the full search state, including explored routes, available alternatives, and properties of intermediates, enabling informed decisions grounded in both global progress and domain knowledge. Experiments on in-distribution and out-of-distribution benchmarks demonstrate that RetroAgent delivers strong performance and generalization.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM agent with structured memory for retrosynthesis planning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于LLM的智能体结合结构化记忆进行逆合成规划，可类比用于药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于LLM的智能体结合结构化记忆进行逆合成规划，可类比用于药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于LLM的智能体结合结构化记忆进行逆合成规划，可类比用于药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RetroAgent is an LLM agent that bridges symbolic search and neural reasoning for retrosynthesis planning, demonstrating multi-agent capabilities applicable to DDI prediction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多步逆合成规划面临组合搜索空间大的挑战。本文提出RetroAgent，一个利用大语言模型和结构化记忆的智能体，它通过记忆和化学工具进行符号搜索与神经推理的桥接，从而高效规划逆合成路径。该工作展示了LLM智能体在化学推理中的潜力，其多智能体协作和结构化记忆机制可迁移至药物相互作用预测中的知识检索与推理任务。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多步逆合成规划面临组合搜索空间大的挑战。本文提出RetroAgent，一个利用大语言模型和结构化记忆的智能体，它通过记忆和化学工具进行符号搜索与神经推理的桥接，从而高效规划逆合成路径。该工作展示了LLM智能体在化学推理中的潜力，其多智能体协作和结构化记忆机制可迁移至药物相互作用预测中的知识检索与推理任务。

quick_skim.title: SpatialJEPA: JEPA-inspired graph-context distillation for spatially aware multiomics integration
quick_skim.abstract: Computational frameworks for integrating spatial genomics modalities extend cell-based representation learning across molecular layers, but many paired RNA-ATAC datasets are dissociated and lack spatial coordinates. We introduce SpatialJEPA, a JEPA-inspired teacher-student framework for transferring spatial context from spatial multiomics data to non-spatial multiome data. In contrast to patch- or feature-masking objectives, SpatialJEPA masks spatial context by replacing the teacher's spatial neighborhood graph with a self-only identity graph during student training, making the spatial sample appear dissociated to the student. The student learns to match teacher embeddings from this graph-context-restricted view and can therefore be applied to dissociated RNA-ATAC data at inference time. In mouse brain multiomics, the resulting representation supports source-target alignment, recovers spatially organized transcriptomic and chromatin-accessibility programs, and shows concordance with ligand-receptor pathway structure compared with non-spatial references. Accepted at the CIBB 2026 conference (https://cibb2026.teralab.ai/)
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 教师-学生框架用于图上下文蒸馏，可迁移至药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 教师-学生框架用于图上下文蒸馏，可迁移至药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 教师-学生框架用于图上下文蒸馏，可迁移至药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SpatialJEPA uses a JEPA-inspired teacher-student framework to transfer spatial context from spatial multiomics data to non-spatial data via graph-context distillation, a method applicable to DDI knowledge transfer.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多组学整合中空间信息缺失是一个常见问题。本文提出SpatialJEPA，一种基于JEPA的教师-学生框架，通过图上下文蒸馏将空间多组学数据的空间上下文迁移到非空间数据中。学生网络学习匹配教师网络在屏蔽空间邻居图后的嵌入，从而实现对空间上下文的建模。该方法为知识迁移和结构蒸馏提供了新思路，可应用于冷启动药物相互作用的表示学习。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多组学整合中空间信息缺失是一个常见问题。本文提出SpatialJEPA，一种基于JEPA的教师-学生框架，通过图上下文蒸馏将空间多组学数据的空间上下文迁移到非空间数据中。学生网络学习匹配教师网络在屏蔽空间邻居图后的嵌入，从而实现对空间上下文的建模。该方法为知识迁移和结构蒸馏提供了新思路，可应用于冷启动药物相互作用的表示学习。
